objective: Turn OpenCode setup proof and autopilot risk into tracked guide clicks, Pro checkout starts, and qualified workflow-hardening conversations.
headline: Turn OpenCode autopilot demand into proof-backed guardrail installs.
shortDescription: ThumbGate gives OpenCode a repo-local profile, a portable MCP adapter, and local-first Pre-Action Checks that block repeated tool-call mistakes before the next run.
summary: OpenCode demand in ThumbGate should start with repo-native install proof and unattended-run risk, then split into Pro for solo operators or Workflow Hardening Sprint for rollout-critical workflows.

canonicalIdentity.githubDescription: Agent governance for ThumbGate: 👍/👎 become Pre-Action Checks that block repeat mistakes before code, money, or customer systems change.

surfaces.name: Multica + OpenCode autopilot guide
surfaces.operatorUse: Primary acquisition surface for OpenCode users who already run scheduled jobs, autopilot loops, or unattended agent work.
surfaces.buyerSignal: Autopilot owners who already understand repeated tool-call risk and need a guardrail before the next unattended run.

surfaces.name: Repo-native OpenCode integration guide
surfaces.operatorUse: Use when the buyer wants repo-local proof that ThumbGate already ships OpenCode-specific configuration, worktree safety, and review boundaries.
surfaces.buyerSignal: Builders who need to see the actual OpenCode config surface before trusting a generic setup pitch.

surfaces.name: Portable OpenCode MCP profile install
surfaces.operatorUse: Use when the buyer wants the portable MCP profile for another OpenCode project or a global config path outside this repo.
surfaces.buyerSignal: Self-serve OpenCode evaluators who care about a version-pinned profile and explicit install instructions before they commit time.

surfaces.name: Proof-backed setup guide
surfaces.operatorUse: General self-serve activation surface once the OpenCode buyer accepts the install path and wants proof plus pricing guardrails in one place.
surfaces.buyerSignal: Operators who want one proof-backed route into install, Pro, or workflow hardening without drifting into unsupported claims.

followOnOffers.label: ThumbGate Pro
followOnOffers.buyer: Solo OpenCode operators who already proved one blocked repeat and now want the personal dashboard plus proof-ready exports.

followOnOffers.label: Workflow Hardening Sprint
followOnOffers.pricing: Intake-led sprint, then Team at $49/seat/mo with 3-seat minimum after qualification
followOnOffers.buyer: Teams running OpenCode or adjacent agent workflows with one repeated failure, one owner, and one approval boundary.

operatorQueue.audience: OpenCode or Multica operator running scheduled jobs or autopilot workflows
operatorQueue.evidence: The Multica guide explicitly shows OpenCode as a scheduled-job runtime, and current report workflow-control targets: 0.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: They can name one unattended run, destructive tool call, or approval-boundary failure they want blocked before the next schedule fires.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Autopilot guide -> pain confirmation -> Workflow Hardening Sprint.

operatorQueue.audience: OpenCode builder who wants repo-local guardrails and a portable MCP profile
operatorQueue.evidence: The repo ships both opencode.json and a portable profile. Current explicit OpenCode mentions in the ranked queue: 0.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: They want config proof or a version-pinned install path before they decide whether the free setup is enough.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Integration doc -> setup guide -> Pro after one blocked repeat is real.

operatorQueue.audience: Agent-tooling maintainer evaluating a self-serve install before a services conversation
operatorQueue.evidence: Current report self-serve target count: 0; production-style targets: 0.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: One repeated mistake still survives the install path, or one rollout workflow clearly needs approval boundaries and proof.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Setup guide first; Pro for solo proof, Sprint for rollout risk.

outreachDrafts.draft: ThumbGate already ships a repo-local OpenCode profile plus a portable MCP install path. If you want the shortest proof-backed route, start with the setup guide here: https://thumbgate-production.up.railway.app/guide?utm_source=opencode&utm_medium=operator_outreach&utm_campaign=opencode_outreach_setup&utm_content=guide&campaign_variant=repo_native_setup&offer_code=OPENCODE-OUTREACH_SETUP&cta_id=opencode_outreach_setup&cta_placement=outreach_draft&surface=opencode_outreach . If one repeated agent mistake still survives after that, Pro is the clean next step instead of a generic services pitch.

outreachDrafts.audience: Solo operator who already confirmed one repeated OpenCode failure
outreachDrafts.draft: Now that the repeat is concrete, send proof before the paid lane: https://github.com/IgorGanapolsky/ThumbGate/blob/main/docs/VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md . If the buyer wants the personal dashboard, export-ready evidence, and a clean self-serve path after that proof, move to Pro here: https://thumbgate-production.up.railway.app/checkout/pro?utm_source=opencode&utm_medium=operator_outreach&utm_campaign=opencode_outreach_pro&utm_content=pro&campaign_variant=proof_after_pain&offer_code=OPENCODE-OUTREACH_PRO&cta_id=opencode_outreach_pro&cta_placement=outreach_draft&plan_id=pro&surface=opencode_outreach .

If OpenCode is already running scheduled jobs or unattended workflows, the missing piece is not another prompt. It is a gate before the next risky tool call. The Multica/OpenCode guide is here: https://thumbgate-production.up.railway.app/guides/multica-thumbgate-setup?utm_source=opencode&utm_medium=operator_outreach&utm_campaign=opencode_outreach_autopilot&utm_content=guide&campaign_variant=scheduled_jobs&offer_code=OPENCODE-OUTREACH_AUTOPILOT&cta_id=opencode_outreach_autopilot&cta_placement=outreach_draft&surface=opencode_outreach . If one repeated failure or approval boundary is already costing you time, route it into the Workflow Hardening Sprint here: https://thumbgate-production.up.railway.app/?utm_source=opencode&utm_medium=operator_outreach&utm_campaign=opencode_outreach_sprint&utm_content=workflow_sprint&campaign_variant=autopilot_risk&offer_code=OPENCODE-OUTREACH_SPRINT&cta_id=opencode_outreach_sprint&cta_placement=outreach_draft&surface=opencode_outreach#workflow-sprint-intake .

measurementPlan.policy: Treat OpenCode guide visits as acquisition evidence only after a tracked proof click, Pro checkout start, or qualified workflow-hardening conversation exists.
measurementPlan.minimumUsefulSignal: One tracked Pro checkout start or one qualified workflow-hardening conversation sourced from an OpenCode-tagged surface.
measurementPlan.strongSignal: Three tracked paid-intent events across Pro checkout starts or sprint conversations.
guardrails: Do not claim installs, marketplace approval, or revenue without direct command evidence.
Do not lead first-touch outreach with proof links before the buyer confirms pain.
Keep proof claims aligned with VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md.

milestones.goal: Push the repo-native OpenCode setup story and the Multica autopilot guide into one consistent proof-backed lane.
milestones.decisionRule: Do not widen the pitch unless setup or autopilot clicks start producing pain-confirmed replies.

milestones.goal: Promote whichever OpenCode motion converts first: solo Pro or workflow hardening.
milestones.decisionRule: If proof clicks exist without paid intent, move proof and follow-on offers higher in the setup and autopilot copy.

milestones.goal: Decide whether OpenCode stays a self-serve lane or earns more direct founder-led outbound.
milestones.decisionRule: Only increase OpenCode-specific outbound after there is qualified conversation or paid-intent evidence.

doNotCountAsSuccess: proof clicks without a tracked paid-intent event
